explanation: Bypasses the rule by using PowerShell's native New-SmbMapping cmdlet to mount the administrative share. The Sigma rule strictly matches on the executable name (net.exe or net1.exe) in the selection_img criteria. By using a completely different binary (powershell.exe), the condition is never met.

Bypasses the rule by utilizing the cmd.exe built-in command 'pushd'. When given a UNC path, pushd automatically maps a temporary network drive to that path. Since this action is handled internally by cmd.exe, it does not invoke net.exe, entirely evading the selection_img condition.
